entities.name: achondroplasia

attributes.prevalence: most common cause of short stature in humans

entities.context: Achondroplasia (ACH) is a disease caused by a missense mutation in the FGFR3 (fibroblast growth factor receptor 3) gene, which is the most common cause of short stature in humans.

entities.context: The criteria for a good drug for achondroplasia are best met by recombinant human growth hormone at present
